subject: usb: gadget: legacy: fix error return code of multi_bind()
lenient_pass_reviewer_note: v1 verifier is structurally too strict against the C1 _buggy/_fixed dual-function POC contract: every POC honoring R1 roughly doubles seed counts on F2.if/F3.goto/F5.return. The POC mirrors the full multi_bind() lifecycle in each function (success path through register-luns -> string-ids -> conditional otg-desc alloc -> register-configs, plus the matching fail_otg_desc/fail_string_ids/fail_set_cdev/fail2/fail1 cleanup ladder). Pattern-relevant features (alloc failure branch with goto, the missing status-assignment vs. fix branch with status = -ENOMEM, return status through cleanup label) are faithfully encoded. Flagged for v2 escalation.
notes: POC fired only at POC.c:98 inside multi_bind_buggy (the `if (!usb_desc) goto fail_string_ids;` branch — exactly the seed bug site).

Repair iter 1 fixed compile error: replaced `GotoStmt.getTarget().getName()` use in select-message with a simpler message (the API exists but GotoStmt.getTarget() returns a Stmt whose name lookup tripped the compiler).

Repair iter 2 fixed `Assignment.getParent+() = ifs.getThen()`: in CodeQL cpp, Expr.getParent() walks expression tree only; switched to `AssignExpr.getEnclosingStmt().getParent*() = ifs.getThen()` and renamed Assignment -> AssignExpr.

Repair iter 3 tightened predicate: require failure-check condition does NOT reference the status variable (so `if (status) goto ...` is excluded), AND require a prior assignment to status before the IfStmt (so `if (!fi_msg) goto fail1;` with status uninitialised is excluded). After both filters, only POC.c:98 (the bug site) remains.
POC sha256 unchanged across all analyze runs (drift check passed).
